question: How do I fix a dripping faucet in my kitchen sink?
answer: Start by turning off the water supply valves under the sink. Place a bucket beneath the faucet to catch residual water. Remove the faucet handle by unscrewing the set screw, then pull off the handle. Use a wrench to remove the packing nut and pull out the stem. Inspect the washer at the bottom of the stem — if worn or cracked, replace it with a matching size. Reassemble in reverse order and slowly turn the water back on to test.
steps: Turn off water supply valves under the sink
Place a bucket under the faucet to catch drips
Remove the faucet handle by unscrewing the set screw
Use a wrench to loosen and remove the packing nut
Pull out the stem and inspect the washer
Replace the worn washer with a matching size
Reassemble the faucet in reverse order
Slowly turn the water back on and check for leaks
safety_info: Always turn off the water supply fully before disassembly. Do not overtighten fittings — this can crack the seat. Dry the area before working to avoid slipping.
tips: Take a photo of the faucet assembly before disassembly
Bring the old washer to the hardware store to match the size exactly
Apply plumber's grease to the new washer before installing
